summary: PR 2319 replaced four fighter TODO stubs with C implementations across Kirby, Crazy Hand, common grab escape, and fighter color overlay code, plus corrected a Crazy Hand attribute field from s32 to float. Bot output supports three new 100% function matches and one Kirby function improvement to 95.54%, while the PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions.

key_files.role: Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420, iterating Kirby copy-hat visibility lookup entries and updating material diffuse color through DObj/MObj/Material pointers.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot reports ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 improved to 95.54%

key_files.role: Implemented ftCh_Init_80157170, setting Crazy Hand damage motion values, changing to ftMh_MS_Damage2, advancing animation, and playing SFX.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Crazy Hand heavy damage flinch description

key_files.role: Changed ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24 type from s32 to float to support float use in ftCh_Init_80157170.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCrazyHandAttributes x24 s32 to float

key_files.role: Implemented fn_800DC070, a grabbed fighter CaptureJump escape transition that sets self velocity, clears buryjump state, notifies victim, and changes motion state.
Implemented ft_800C0098, clearing fp->x508 color animation and conditionally applying Cloaking Device/spycloak color animation; wrapped in auto_inline/global_optimizer pragmas.

what_changed: Replaced TODO marker for fn_800DC070 with a function that uses GET_FIGHTER, victim_gobj, p_ftCommonData velocity fields, ftCo_800DC920, and Fighter_ChangeMotionState to enter ftCo_MS_CaptureJump.
Replaced TODO marker for ftCh_Init_80157170 with a function using ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs, Crazy Hand motion variables, ftMh_MS_Damage2, ftAnim_8006EBA4, and ft_PlaySFX.
Changed ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24 from s32 to float.
Replaced TODO marker for ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 with nested loops over FtPartsVisLookup/TempS entries to update Kirby copied hat material diffuse color.
Replaced TODO marker for ft_800C0098 with color-animation clearing/reapply logic and surrounded it with #pragma push, #pragma auto_inline off, #pragma global_optimizer off, and #pragma pop.
Bot report recorded 464 matched-code bytes total, with three new 100% matches and one unmatched-item improvement.

smart_moves.text: The Kirby implementation uses structured nested loops over lookup counts and entry counts rather than duplicating repeated access blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 for loops over lookup->x0 and entry->x0

smart_moves.text: The Crazy Hand attribute type was updated in the owning type definition when the implementation used x24 as a float value.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h x24 s32 to float

smart_moves.text: The ftcolanim pragma workaround was tightly scoped with push/pop around only ft_800C0098.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push before ft_800C0098 and #pragma pop after function

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched fighter function proves a field is consumed as a float, update the struct field type instead of compensating with casts or raw offset access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24 changed from s32 to float
raw_diff: attrs->x24 assigned into vector-like motion field

decomp_lessons.lesson: Nested lookup tables for fighter parts can often be expressed with natural loops over entry counts and pointer/index iteration while retaining typed DObj/MObj/Material accesses.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_DObj, HSD_MObj, HSD_Material typed accesses

decomp_lessons.lesson: Pragmas may be part of a historical match, but the PR evidence here only shows their use and tight scoping, not why they were required; curator should not generalize this as normal style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: auto_inline off and global_optimizer off around ft_800C0098

naming_conventions.name: fp

evidence_refs: raw_diff: Fighter* fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj) in multiple implementations

naming_conventions.meaning: grab victim game object from fp->victim_gobj

naming_conventions.name: attrs
naming_conventions.meaning: Crazy Hand external attribute pointer from fp->ft_data->ext_attr

naming_conventions.name: lookup, entry
naming_conventions.meaning: Kirby parts visibility lookup table and per-entry record locals
evidence_refs: raw_diff: FtPartsVisLookup* lookup and TempS* entry in ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used natural nested for loops with explicit int counters and pointer increment for Kirby hat material traversal.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (j = 0; j < entry->x0; j++, p++)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used typed object chain accesses where available: DObj to MObj to Material, Fighter fields, and Crazy Hand attribute struct.
Scoped #pragma auto_inline off and #pragma global_optimizer off around ft_800C0098.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: No PR comment or body text explains the pragma necessity or adjacent-function checks.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used an assignment expression as a Fighter_ChangeMotionState argument in ftCh_Init_80157170.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fp->mv.ch.unk0.xC.z = ftCh_Init_804DA070 passed as the start-frame argument
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Evidence supports that this was the submitted source shape, but not that it is a broadly reusable style.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, merge time, changed files
human_pr_text: author summary and claimed verification
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for five files
notes: There are no review comments and no human review feedback in the loaded slice.

The PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, but the bot report lists only three new 100% matches and Kirby ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 as 95.54%; this intake preserves the discrepancy instead of treating all four as verified 100% matches.

The use of #pragma global_optimizer off and #pragma auto_inline off is visible in the diff, but the loaded PR evidence does not explain why they were required or whether adjacent regressions were checked.
The raw PR JSON is truncated, but loaded human text, diff, changed_files, and text_corpus are sufficient for compact intake.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Struct field type recovery can accompany a function match
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2319 matched ftCh_Init_80157170 and changed ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24 from s32 to float, with the implementation assigning attrs->x24 into a motion/vector-like float field. Candidate lesson: when a decompiled body proves the scalar type used by an attribute field, update the owning type rather than adding casts or raw offset workarounds.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Natural nested loops for fighter part lookup traversal
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2319 expresses Kirby copy-hat diffuse-color updates as nested loops over FtPartsVisLookup and TempS counts, using typed HSD_DObj/HSD_MObj/HSD_Material accesses and null checks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 nested loops and typed object chain

source_update_candidates.title: PR 2319 touched fighter matching paths
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2319 changed fighter matching code in ftCommon Attack100, ftCrazyHand init/types, ftKirby init, and ftcolanim.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could support path-level indexing for fighter decomp examples; curator should decide whether this belongs in path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Crazy Hand attribute x24 appears to be float in PR 2319
source_update_candidates.text: The PR changed ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24 from s32 to float and used attrs->x24 in ftCh_Init_80157170. This may be a data/type-sheet candidate, but the PR evidence alone does not prove semantic name or broader attribute meaning.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential typed-field fact for curator review; do not promote beyond the observed type change.

source_update_candidates.title: Pragma exceptions need explicit evidence in PR descriptions
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2319 used #pragma auto_inline off and #pragma global_optimizer off around ft_800C0098, but the loaded PR evidence does not explain necessity or adjacent regression checks. This reinforces the existing avoid-pragmas-register-asm and matching-tactics-need-evidence standards rather than proving a new accepted pattern.
source_update_candidates.reason: Curator may want this PR as an example/caution under existing standards, not as permission for broad pragma use.

rejection_notes: Do not record that all four functions were verified 100% matched: the author body says this, but the bot report contradicts it for ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 with 95.54%.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale or requested changes; no review comments are loaded.
Do not infer semantic names for Crazy Hand attribute x24/x28 or Fighter color globals Fighter_804D6538/Fighter_804D653C from this PR alone.
Do not generalize #pragma global_optimizer off as acceptable normal decomp style; the PR lacks explicit justification beyond the merged diff.
